{{Chess Opening Theory/Position |Stafford gambit accepted |eco=[[Chess/ECOC|C42]] |parent=[[../|Stafford gambit]] }} == 4. Nxc6 · Stafford gambit accepted == White, being up one pawn in material, trades knights. [[/4...dxc6|'''4...dxc6''']] and Black recaptures while opening lines for their bishop and queen. Black will want to make an aggressive attack on White's kingside and try to swindle a win before the end game. Taking back the other way, [[/4...bxc6|'''4...bxc6''']], doesn't give Black developmental compensation and Black cannot then justify the pawn sacrifice. =Theory table= {{ChessTable}}. '''1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nf6 3. Nxe5 Nc6 4. Nxc6'''
